title: The Unclean Spirit Silenced
subtitle: In the synagogue at Capernaum, the Lord casts out the demon and is confessed even by hell

stillness: Be still, and enter the house of God.
If paradise be in this present life, it is in the cloister or in the Scriptures.
Nothing in this life is felt more sweetly, nothing taken up more eagerly, than the study of sacred Scripture.
Quiet your servile works; make room for the things of the spirit.
gospel: and at once, on the Sabbath, He went into the synagogue and taught them.
And they were all astonished at His doctrine,
for He was teaching them as one having power, and not as the Scribes.
And there was in their synagogue a man having an unclean spirit,
and he cried out with a loud voice, saying:
What have we to do with You, Jesus of Nazareth?
Have You come to destroy us before the time?
I know who You are: the Holy One of God.
And Jesus rebuked him, saying: Be silent, and come out of the man.
And the demon, throwing him into the midst and tearing him,
and crying out with a loud voice, came out of him,
behold: Attend to each thing, as if you were present.
Behold the Lord Jesus entering the synagogue, where the learned in the Law gather to teach and the people to hear.
Behold the crowd stunned: He had not learned the Law and the Prophets, yet He teaches most clearly,
and He confirms His teaching by divine works, while the Scribes only speak and fear to tell the truth.
Behold now, brought perhaps to that holy place that he might be freed, the man held fast by the demon.
Hear him scream with a great voice, for the devil, stirred by envy, is tortured at the salvation of men.
See the unclean spirit confess what is true — yet the Lord commands him: Be silent.
Behold the demon hurl the man into their midst, tearing him, and go out — yet harming him not at all.
consider: The devil grieved at Christ's doctrine, for it was the salvation and the medicine of souls.
As Theophilus says: the demon called it his own perdition to go out from the man.
Merciless toward men, the demons reckon they suffer some evil when they cease to torment us.
The demons knew Him to be the Christ promised in the Law; yet they did not know Him to be God.
For had they known, never would they have led the Jews to crucify Him.
See how perverse are many men, who in their adversity blaspheme God — while the very demons proclaim Him.
This confession is not of the will, which no reward follows; it is the wringing of necessity, which forces the unwilling.
As fugitive slaves, seeing their master after long time, suspect nothing but the lash —
so the demons, beholding the Lord, believed He had come to judge them.
The presence of the Savior is the torment of the demons.
He is called the unclean spirit from his effect: he makes unclean the one he possesses.
And Jesus rebuked him, lest he mingle false things with true, and lest He seem to seek testimony from him.
Here a saving teaching is given us: believe not the demons, who are liars and stood not in the truth,
however much they declare the truth; for finding any who believe them, they mingle a lie with the truth.
And when the devil casts a man's sin into the midst, to his shame —
and the sinner sees himself confounded — then he lets the sin go, and the devil is forced to go out from him.
Fill their faces with shame, O Lord, and they shall seek Your name.
For the shame a sinner has of his sin is greatly profitable for the letting go of sin.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, remove and cast out the unclean spirit from me,
that it may in no way be able to stain me, or to hold me fast in stains;
that I may be, and may appear, dead to the world,
once I am freed from that depraved possessor who drives us on through unclean things.
I ask You also, Lord, physician of souls, that You would deign to heal me from the fever of vices,
so that, rising up from them, I may be able to minister to You and to render You a pleasing service.
Heal me too, Lord, and all who are held by the varied languors of the soul,
that, made sound and whole from our sins, we may minister to You and accomplish what is well-pleasing to You. Amen.

latinJewel.english: The presence of the Savior is the torment of the demons.
